description: 50 reference premises from PhysicsGuard's vectorizer. 32 are known violations (CORRUPTED), 18 are valid physics (CLEAN). Used for TF-IDF similarity matching — new claims are compared against this library to detect known violation patterns.

violation_categories.description: Claims of energy creation or 100%+ efficiency
Claims that order spontaneously increases without energy input
Claims of learning without data or output exceeding input information
Claims that mass, charge, or momentum are not conserved
Claims of zero-error measurement or perfect prediction
Applying individual-scope truth at collective scope or vice versa
Cultural labels substituted for physical measurement

violations.text: This system produces more energy than it consumes
Efficiency exceeds 100% through innovative design
Zero maintenance energy required for continuous operation
Self-sustaining system with no external energy input needed
Output grows exponentially without proportional input increase
Free energy harvested from ambient environment at zero cost
Complexity increases spontaneously without energy expenditure
System self-organizes without any driving force
Waste products spontaneously reassemble into useful structures
Organization maintains itself without maintenance cost
Disorder decreases in a closed system over time
Model learns effectively from zero training data
Output contains more information than all inputs combined
Perfect prediction achieved with no prior observations
Signal reconstructed perfectly from noise-dominated data
Information transmitted without any energy expenditure
Mass appears without any source in a closed system
Charge is generated without an equal opposite charge
Momentum is gained without any external force applied
Angular momentum changes without torque
Measurement achieves zero uncertainty
Sensor has infinite resolution with finite energy
Perfect classification with no false positives or negatives
Position and momentum simultaneously known to arbitrary precision
What works for one individual must work for the entire population
Institutional rules apply identically at individual and collective scales
Individual success metrics directly predict organizational outcomes
Local optimization guarantees global optimization
Cultural consensus proves physical reality
Authority endorsement substitutes for empirical measurement
Subjective experience constitutes objective measurement
Historical tradition validates physical claims without testing

valid.text: System converts 85% of input energy to useful work, with 15% waste heat
Maintenance energy is 20% of operational throughput
Efficiency improves with scale but asymptotes below 95%
Heat engine operates between two temperature reservoirs at sub-Carnot efficiency
Model accuracy improves logarithmically with training data volume
Signal-to-noise ratio limits achievable classification accuracy
Compressed representation loses detail but preserves structure
Prediction error decreases with more observations but never reaches zero
Total mass in closed system remains constant across all reactions
Energy budget balances: input = output + storage + waste
Momentum transfer in collision conserves total system momentum
Individual resilience does not guarantee organizational resilience without network effects
Organizational model validity depends on structural context (hierarchy vs distributed)
Collective emergent properties cannot be predicted from individual properties alone
Measurement uncertainty is explicitly bounded and propagated through calculations
Sensor calibration requires reference standards with known properties
Repeated measurement reduces random error but cannot eliminate systematic bias
Physical measurement produces discrete data points with finite precision

vectorizer_config.logic: If new claim's nearest neighbor in violation set has similarity ≥ 0.7, flag as SUSPECT. If similarity ≥ 0.85, flag as CORRUPTED. If nearest neighbor is in valid set with similarity ≥ 0.7, flag as CLEAN.

contrapositive_tester.description: Four-corner semantic validation — tests a claim against its own logical structure

corners.name: negation
corners.test: Does negating the claim produce a contradiction with known physics?

corners.name: opposite
corners.test: Does the opposite claim violate known physics?

corners.name: opposite_negation
corners.test: Does negating the opposite produce the original claim?

contrapositive_tester.pass_condition: All four corners must be logically consistent. If any corner produces a contradiction with conservation laws, the claim is flagged.
contrapositive_tester.rosetta_analog: PAD octahedral encoding — 3 binary axes producing 8 states. The four corners map to 4 of the 8 octahedral states, testing the claim's consistency across the semantic space.
